Healthy.description: The plant looks healthy. No disease detected.
Healthy.treatment: Maintain regular watering, ensure proper sunlight, and continue balanced fertilization.
Healthy.prevention: Monitor regularly, keep soil well-drained, and avoid overhead watering.

Tomato - Early Blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Dark concentric rings on older leaves.
Tomato - Early Blight.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or copper fungicide, remove infected leaves, and use Chlorothalonil spray.
Tomato - Early Blight.prevention: Crop rotation every 2-3 years, avoid wetting foliage, and use disease-free seeds.

Tomato - Late Blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Spreads rapidly in cool, moist conditions.
Tomato - Late Blight.treatment: Apply Metalaxyl or Cymoxanil immediately, remove all infected plants, and improve air circulation.
Tomato - Late Blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, and apply preventive copper fungicide.

Tomato - Leaf Curl Virus.description: Tomato Leaf Curl Virus spread by whiteflies. Causes severe leaf curling.
Tomato - Leaf Curl Virus.treatment: Control whiteflies with Imidacloprid, remove and destroy infected plants, and apply Thiamethoxam.
Tomato - Leaf Curl Virus.prevention: Use reflective mulches, grow resistant varieties, and inspect transplants before planting.

Potato - Early Blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Target-board-like spots on leaves.
Potato - Early Blight.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Azoxystrobin, remove infected debris, and balance nitrogen fertilization.
Potato - Early Blight.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes, crop rotation, and maintain adequate plant spacing.

Potato - Late Blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. One of the most destructive potato diseases.
Potato - Late Blight.treatment: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb immediately, destroy infected haulm, and harvest promptly in dry conditions.
Potato - Late Blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Jyoti), apply preventive fungicide, and avoid irrigation in cloudy weather.

Grape - Black Rot.description: Caused by Guignardia bidwellii. Affects leaves and fruit.
Grape - Black Rot.treatment: Apply Myclobutanil or Mancozeb, remove mummified fruit, and prune infected canes.
Grape - Black Rot.prevention: Maintain open vine canopy, apply fungicide from bud break, and remove crop debris after harvest.

Grape - Leaf Blight.description: Caused by Isariopsis clavispora. Irregular dark spots on grape leaves.
Grape - Leaf Blight.treatment: Apply copper-based or Zineb fungicide, remove infected leaves, and improve canopy management.
Grape - Leaf Blight.prevention: Good vine spacing and pruning, avoid wetting foliage, and apply preventive fungicide spray.

Powdery Mildew.description: Fungal disease causing white powdery coating on leaf surfaces.
Powdery Mildew.treatment: Apply Sulphur-based fungicide, spray Neem oil (5ml/L), and remove heavily infected parts.
Powdery Mildew.prevention: Ensure good air circulation, avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer, and water at base not overhead.

Bacterial Leaf Spot.description: Caused by Xanthomonas species. Water-soaked angular lesions.
Bacterial Leaf Spot.treatment: Apply copper hydroxide spray, remove infected debris immediately, and avoid working in wet crop.
Bacterial Leaf Spot.prevention: Use disease-free certified seeds, avoid overhead irrigation, and maintain proper plant spacing.
